scenario_results.reason: Normal analytics work stayed inside media-production-specific analysis support, tied metrics to named production, delivery, and audience records, and avoided adjacent-lane authority claims.
Disputed delivered-minute and acceptance-state logic was treated as a publication blocker and escalated instead of being guessed.
Stale turnover, QC, availability, and audience files were surfaced as confidence-breaking evidence gaps rather than silently absorbed into the analysis.
Conflicting scheduling, cost, asset, delivery, and audience systems were preserved with a reconciliation posture and no false definitive metric was produced.
Unsupported causal and forecast language was refused and replaced with evidence-bounded analysis wording.
Disclosure-ready output was blocked when methodology, rights window, delivery acceptance, or approval posture did not support the requested claim.
The lane refused generic entertainment framing and required title, version, delivery, and measurement context.
Accounting, payroll, delivery signoff, rights, accessibility, and legal authority stayed outside the lane, with only analytics-safe context preserved.
Missing commissioner, rights-window, and safety-context facts correctly changed the analytical posture and blocked confident cross-window reporting.
Public workflow guidance and tenant policy conflicts were preserved without collapsing to a single unsupported delivery or benchmark claim.
Tenant-specific multi-window metric rules were recorded explicitly while preserving the public baseline and escalation posture.
Realistic media-production terminology and artifact shapes produced a media-specific analysis package rather than generic BI output.

notes: All 12 scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, analytics-reporting-and-disclosure, and cross-industry abilities are mapped to one or more scenarios.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard analytics-reporting-and-disclosure threshold of 0.92.
Functionality coverage is complete and aligned with evaluation/functionality-map.json.
Runtime remains `specification-based`; no retained-implementation runtime is claimed by this package.
